answerBSource: In the tank-top bag
answerCSource: Evenly distributed on the right and left of the handlebars

questionTextSource: How must you protect yourself from serious head injuries?
explanationSource: An officially approved protective helmet is required to protect your head against injuries

answerBSource: - a steel helmet
answerCSource: - a tested protective work helmet

questionTextSource: What applies to the installation and use of rear-view mirrors on motorcycles?
explanationSource: In order to be able to use your rearview mirror, you must ensure that they are well fixed, adjusted, and work (are not cracked, blind)

questionTextSource: What can prevent you from hearing warning signals?
explanationSource: Other sounds, such as a loud radio or a loud exhaust, can prevent car drivers from perceiving important warning signals. Also, the use of headphones usually limits the ability to hear. This often means that warning signals are ignored by drivers. Make sure that you can always hear warning signals.
answerASource: Having the radio on too loud
answerBSource: Using headphones
answerCSource: The exhaust being too loud

questionTextSource: You have sold your vehicle which is still registered. The buyer has paid. What must you do?
explanationSource: If you have sold your licensed vehicle and received the payment, proceed as follows: 1. You hand over all vehicle documents to the new vehicle owner in exchange for a confirmation of receipt. 2. You report the name and address of the vehicle buyer to the admission office. This way, you protect yourself from unjustified claims that could be caused by traffic violations on the part of the new owner. 3. The inspection plate remains on the vehicle.
answerASource: Inform the registration centre immediately of the buyer's name and address
answerBSource: Hand over the vehicle registration certificate (Fahrzeugschein) and vehicle registration book (Fahrzeugbrief) or permit for use (Betriebserlaubnis) to the buyer against receipt
answerCSource: Remove the test stamp from the official registration plate

questionTextSource: What is important with regard to the official registration plates?
explanationSource: For license numbers, it is important that they are placed in the required place. In addition, they must be easy to read and illuminated in the dark.
answerASource: They must be securely attached at the appropriate places
answerBSource: They must always be easily legible
answerCSource: The rear registration plates must be lit up in darkness

questionTextSource: What is required when your vehicle is no longer covered by motor liability insurance?
explanationSource: Without a valid liability insurance, a car is not ready to drive and therefore may not participate in public transport. The liability insurance serves to regulate damage that you may be able to inflict on other vehicles. Irrespective of the financial circumstances of the injuring party, compensation for the injured party is thus guaranteed. If there is no liability insurance, the holder must deregister the car at the admission office. Failure to comply with this rule will result in a penalty.
answerASource: - may no longer be used in road traffic
answerBSource: - must be de-registered at the registration centre
answerCSource: - must be submitted to the registration centre

questionTextSource: What are you allowed to change on the official registration plate?
explanationSource: A license number is an official document, which may not be altered in any way. Therefore, do not attach any foils or stickers to your license plate.
answerASource: Nothing
answerBSource: Attach a protective foil for the lettering
answerCSource: Attach coloured stickers

questionTextSource: As a cyclist, what must you check before commencing your journey?

explanationSource: In blind spots you can not see other road users. Areas around the vehicle that can not be seen even with the vehicle mirrors, count as such. The other two answers are wrong.

questionTextSource: How is it ensured that the rear-view mirror on the bike is working properly?
explanationSource: The rear-view mirror on the two-wheeler must be properly adjusted to the rider - so that he or she can observe all essential traffic. A damaged rearview mirror is not considered to be in proper working order. The rear-view mirror on a motorbike may protrude sideways over the end of the handlebars to provide better coverage of the necessary areas.
answerASource: - must be correctly adjusted
answerBSource: - may not be damaged
answerCSource: - may not extend laterally beyond the handlebars

questionTextSource: When driving, what can cause you to miss warning signals?
explanationSource:
Noise that is too loud, such as listening to music that is too loud, can drown out the warning signal and cause you to react to it too late.
Distractions caused by talking on the phone can also mean that you hear warning signals too late or not at all.
answerASource: Listening to loud music
answerBSource: Becoming distracting during a telephone call
answerCSource: Driving at a low engine speed

questionTextSource: What is the benefit of wearing an integral helmet when riding a motorcycle?
explanationSource: A full-face helmet offers more protection than other helmet models, as full-face helmets are completely closed. The chin forms a whole with the helmet and cannot be pushed up, which means you are well protected against injuries. The complete face protection keeps wind, rain and insects away from your eyes. Your eyes are protected against wind and weather by the visor and your vision is not restricted by the weather.
answerASource: - injuries
answerBSource: - insects
answerCSource: - becoming wet

questionTextSource: What should you check on your two-wheeler before moving off?

answerBSource: The proper functioning of the brakes

questionTextSource: When riding a motorbike, what makes you more visible to other road users?

questionTextSource: What criterion do motorbike boots have to fulfil?
explanationSource:
Pay attention to the exact fit, the boots should fit snugly and reach over your ankles without restricting your freedom of movement.
You should also make sure that the boots have non-slip soles, which provide good grip on the footrests.
answerASource: - have an anti-slip sole
answerBSource: - extend over the ankle
answerCSource: - are loose-fitting

questionTextSource: What criterion do motorbike gloves have to fulfil?
explanationSource:
The motorbike gloves should fit snugly but not be too tight. Your fingers should be slightly bent without the fabric stretching.
The gloves should give you a good grip so that you can operate the brake and clutch levers without any problems.
Choose motorbike gloves made of abrasion-resistant materials such as leather or special textiles in order to be better protected against abrasions and burns in the event of a fall or accident.
answerASource: - are close-fitting
answerBSource: - provide a good grip
answerCSource: - are made from abrasion-resistant material

questionTextSource: You are riding a moped with a maximum design speed of 25 km/h. How do you need to protect yourself from head injuries?

answerBSource: - must protect my head with a suitable and well-fitting bicycle helmet
answerCSource: - dot not have to protect my head when I’m travelling at walking speed

questionTextSource: What should you bear in mind when buying a motorbike jacket and motorbike trousers?
explanationSource:
When purchasing motorcycle jackets and motorcycle pants, you should always pay attention to the safety standard markings so that you are adequately protected.
You should also make sure that the protectors sit exactly in the places they are intended for in order to protect you ideally in the event of a fall.

questionTextSource: What must be remembered with regard to vehicles with canvass covers?

questionTextSource: What must you adjust when driving a vehicle which is not your own?

questionTextSource: How do you secure a car with a manual gearbox against rolling away on a downhill slope?
explanationSource: You can prevent a car with a manual transmission from rolling away by engaging a small gear, such as first gear or reverse gear. Use the parking brake and do not place the gearstick in the neutral position.
answerASource: Apply the parking brake
answerBSource: Engage first gear or reverse gear
answerCSource: Put the gear lever in neutral position

questionTextSource: How do you secure a car with automatic transmission against rolling away on a downhill slope?
explanationSource: You can secure a car with automatic transmission against rolling away by activating the parking brake and additionally bringing the selector lever into the parking position 'P'. You may not select the position 'N', as this is the same as idling in a vehicle with manual transmission.
answerASource: Apply the parking brake
answerBSource: Put the selector lever in the "P" position
answerCSource: Put the selector lever in the "N" position

questionTextSource: What must you do when reversing?

questionTextSource: What should be observed when positioning the head rests in your car?
explanationSource: With correctly adjusted headrests the car driver reduces the force on the cervical spine in case of sudden vehicle movements. This makes headrests a direct protection device for the protection of the vehicle occupants in accidents. The headrests that can be adjusted electrically or manually in height and inclination should be adjusted so that the crown height of the head coincides with the top edge of the headrest. In order for their protective function to be fully effective, you should tilt the headrest so that your back of the head is no more than a few centimeters away from it.
answerASource: The top of the head should be level with the upper edge of the head rest
answerBSource: The spacing between the rear of the head and the head rests should be as small as possible
answerCSource: The upper edge of the head rest should conjoin with the upper neck vertebra

questionTextSource: When should you refrain from using the telephone even if you have a hands-free device?
explanationSource: Despite the availability of a hands-free system, you are likely to become nervous and anxious during difficult conversations. You will pay more attention to the person you are talking to and concentrate less on the road. Distraction can cause you to miss something in traffic and not be able to react quickly enough. You should also avoid using your phone in difficult traffic situations.

answerBSource: In complicated traffic conditions

questionTextSource: You want to use a smartphone mounted on your motor vehicle as a navigation device. What should you observe?

questionTextSource: You are planning a long journey with your motor vehicle. Before commencing the journey, what should you check on the vehicle to avoid endangering yourself and others?
explanationSource:
You should check your tyres at regular intervals, because wear, damage or incorrectly adjusted air pressure as well as insufficient tread depth represent a major safety risk.
A minimum tread depth of 1.6 mm applies. 
The headlights should also be checked for defects and their correct setting. For example, headlights that are set too high can dazzle oncoming traffic. 
A clear view should be ensured on every journey. This includes not only ensuring that the windscreen is free of dirt, but also that there is still sufficient windscreen washer fluid.
answerASource: The tyres
answerBSource: The headlamp setting
answerCSource: That the windscreen wipers are working properly

questionTextSource: What is the main function of the headrest in a car?

questionTextSource: While driving, the inside of your vehicle’s windscreen begins to mist over. How can you counteract this?

questionTextSource: Your car is snowed in. What do you need to do before you start your journey?
explanationSource:
Before you drive off, you must remove the snow from the entire vehicle as it could fall while driving and endanger other road users. In addition, the snow could slide onto your windshield when you brake, blocking your visibility and making you completely unable to act.  Also remove the snow from the lighting equipment so that other road users can see you in good time and you have adequate visibility even in dark lighting conditions. 
Also make sure that no snow covers your license plates. If the license plates are illegible, you risk an administrative offense and a fine.
answerASource: - remove snow from number plate
answerBSource: - remove snow from the vehicle’s roof
answerCSource: - remove snow from the lighting equipment

questionTextSource: Which parts of your motor vehicle do you have to completely clear of snow and ice before driving off?

answerCSource: I have to check that the load is secure

questionTextSource: You have installed a front-mounted, forward-extending attachment to your motor vehicle. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: Because of your extensive forward front attachment you need to consider while driving the modified vehicle dimensions. can ward where visibility is restricted by an appropriate person and dangerous parts of the front mounted implement effective disguise. All three are correct.
answerASource: - arrange for a suitable person to direct me in situations where my visibility is impaired
answerBSource: - take the modified vehicle dimensions into account while driving
answerCSource: - effectively cover the dangerous parts of the front-mounted attachment

questionTextSource: You want to continue your journey after taking a break at a service station. What should you check before recommencing your journey?
explanationSource: After ending a break, you should make sure that there are no loose objects in the footwell. You should also point out and check that all passengers have sat on their seat. Also check that all doors are closed correctly.
answerASource: - there are no loose objects in my footwell
answerBSource: - all doors are closed correctly
answerCSource: - all passengers are seated

questionTextSource: How can you clear the snow and ice from the structure of your vehicle?
explanationSource: When de-icing the vehicle, it is essential to use suitable aids such as de-icing bridges or de-icing ladders with sufficient fall protection to minimize the risk of falls.  Pressing against the tarpaulin structure from the inside with a suitable tool makes it possible to loosen ice and snow without having to climb onto the roof, which also reduces the risk of accidents.
answerASource: By using a de-icing platform
answerBSource: By using a deicing ladder with adequate fall protection
answerCSource: By pressing against the tarpaulin structure from the inside with a suitable implement (e.g. a broom)

questionTextSource: What must you remember in terms of lighting when you wish to attach a trailer?
explanationSource: Regarding the lighting, you should attached to a trailer, you should make sure that the voltage of tractor and trailer is the same and plug and socket must be properly connected. And extends the trailer sideways more than 40 cm above the running lights of the tractor addition, he must have his own position lamps.
answerASource: The trailer must have its own side lights if it extends laterally more than 40 cm beyond the side lights of the truck tractor
answerBSource: Truck tractor and trailer must have the same electric tension. Plug and socket must be properly connected
answerCSource: The electric tension of the truck tractor and trailer may be different if the tension of the trailer is lower

questionTextSource: The tarpaulin of your trailer is frozen over. What could happen when you start a journey?
explanationSource: When the tarp is iced up your trailer, it can happen that can be severely damaged by falling ice sheets during braking air and electrical lines on the car, people are critically injured and other vehicles will be significantly damaged. Please remove the ice from the tarpaulin.
answerASource: - serious damage to air lines and electric cables when braking
answerBSource: - people to suffer life-threatening injuries
answerCSource: - severely damage other vehicles

questionTextSource: A two-axle trailer with overrun brakes and fifth-wheel steering is parked on a slope. How must it be secured against rolling?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: By engaging the parking brake
answerBSource: By placing a wheel chock under a rear wheel
answerCSource: By supporting the forked draw-bar at the height of the coupling

questionTextSource: You have mounted twin tyres on your truck tractor. What must you remember?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Additional lights must be mounted on the outside in darkness if the tyres extend laterally more than 40 cm beyond the lights
answerBSource: If the width of the truck tractor exceeds 2.75 m due to the twin tyres, the excessive width must be marked by warning plates
answerCSource: The width of the truck tractor must not exceed 2.55 m, also with twin tyres

questionTextSource: In what situations may I allow a cyclist to be pulled along?
explanationSource: Pulling along cyclists is dangerous for both you and the cyclist and is not allowed
answerASource: - in no circumstances
answerBSource: - if I drive no faster than walking speed
answerCSource: - if I am driving on less-frequented roads

questionTextSource: As a moped driver (not a light moped), what precautions must you take against head injuries?
explanationSource: To protect your head against injury, an adequate helmet is required. A cyclist or construction helmet is not legal nor sufficient.
answerASource: - a suitable crash helmet
answerBSource: - a certified helmet for cyclists
answerCSource: - a certified construction site helmet

questionTextSource: Who is prohibited from using a mobile phone without a hands-free unit when driving?
explanationSource: Car drivers are allowed to use mobile and car telephones while driving only with hands-free equipment. As soon as you need to pick up or hold the phone to make the call, it is forbidden to make a call while driving.
answerASource: - cars
answerBSource: - trucks
answerCSource: - motorcycles

questionTextSource: You want to attach a device to your tractor. In what situations do you have to be particularly careful?
explanationSource: Especially cautious you should be when the attachments through your vehicle far back. Or even far beyond stand forward and protrude sideways. Again, all three right.
answerASource: - laterally projects beyond the side of the vehicle
answerBSource: - projects far to the rear of the vehicle
answerCSource: - projects far to the front of the vehicle

questionTextSource: When riding a motorbike, how can you help other road users to see you better and as early as possible?
explanationSource: Wearing highly visible clothes (conspicuous clothes) and retro-reflective clothes at night will increase your visibility to other people on the road. Wearing dark clothes with no retro-reflective clothes makes you hard to see on the road.
answerASource: By wearing conspicuous clothing
answerBSource: By wearing retro-reflective clothing after dark
answerCSource: By driving with the main beam light on

questionTextSource: What items form part of your protective clothing when riding a motorbike?
explanationSource: Wearing highly visible clothes (conspicuous clothes) and retro-reflective clothes at night will increase your visibility to other people on the road. Wearing dark clothes with no retro-reflective clothes makes you hard to see on the road.
answerASource: Suitable crash helmet
answerBSource: Suitable gloves
answerCSource: Suitable tank bag

questionTextSource: What items increase safety when riding a motorbike?
explanationSource: Special motorcycle clothing (highly visible, retro-reflective gear at night for example) and a back protector will increase your safety and diminish the risk of injury
answerASource: Back protectors
answerBSource: Special motorcycling clothing
answerCSource: Top case

questionTextSource: You have fitted your motorbike with a tank bag and side panniers. What must you ensure?
explanationSource: When loading your motorcycle with bags and panniers, make sure that you do not exceed the gross vehicle weight limit, that you adjust the rear wheel suspension as well as tire pressure due to additional weight
answerASource: The gross vehicle weight rating may not be exceeded
answerBSource: The tyre inflation pressure should be adjusted to the larger loaded weight
answerCSource: The rear wheel suspension should be adjusted to the larger load if necessary

questionTextSource: You want to drive your agricultural tractor with attached accessory equipment. What must you be aware of before driving off?
explanationSource: Put the implements in transport position and secure them so that they do not pose a danger. Observe the maximum permissible axle loads.
answerASource: The accessory equipment must be put into the transport position and secured there
answerBSource: The maximum gross axle weight rating must be observed
answerCSource: The pedals of the single-wheeled brakes must be disengaged

questionTextSource: You are driving an agricultural tractor with a mounted implement. After finishing working in the field, you want to drive on to a public road. What must you check first?
explanationSource: You must check the transport position of the implement for road travel, as well as the connection device between the tractor and the implement. Furthermore, check the condition and function of the lighting equipment.
answerASource: The coupling device between the tractor and the mounted implement
answerBSource: The transport position of the mounted implement for driving on roads
answerCSource: The condition and proper functioning of the lighting systems

questionTextSource: You are driving a tractor with an attachment. After working in a field, now you want to drive on a public road. What should you note first?
explanationSource: Before driving a tractor with a mounted implement on a road, check that the implement is in the transport position. In addition, the pedals for the individual wheel brakes must be locked and the necessary warning signs must be attached to indicate the width of the vehicle.
answerASource: The foot pedals are locked for the single wheel brake
answerBSource: The attachment is in its transport position
answerCSource: The necessary warning panels are attached to indicate the vehicle width

questionTextSource: You come to a stop unexpectedly on a pedestrian crossing. What do you do?
explanationSource: To avoid danger for pedestrians, pedestrian crossings must be kept clear. Therefore, you need to clear the crossing as soon as possible. Drive forwards or backwards as far as the traffic conditions allow. Pay attention to other road users. Do not drive on the sidewalk. This is intended exclusively for pedestrians.
answerASource: Move slightly forward or backward in order to clear the crossing as quickly as possible
answerBSource: Move half onto the footpath in order to clear the crossing
answerCSource: Reverse under no circumstances, even though the roadway is clear behind you

questionTextSource: What may result from taking part in illegal road races?
explanationSource: Participation in illegal road races can lead to serious traffic accidents and can result in a custodial sentence for the driver and the revocation of the driver's licence.
answerASource: The occurrence of serious road traffic accidents
answerBSource: The imposition of a term of imprisonment on the vehicle driver
answerCSource: The confiscation of the vehicle driver's driving licence

questionTextSource: A 2.50 m wide motor vehicle is loaded with bulky goods projecting laterally more than 50 cm over the side and rear lights. What must be remembered when transporting the load?
explanationSource: If your load 50 cm extends laterally beyond the front and rear position lamps, please make sure to take the charge forward and backward marked and carry the special permission of the Road Safety Authority.
answerASource: The extra-wide load must be marked to the front and rear
answerBSource: A special permit from the road traffic licensing authority must be carried
answerCSource: If an amber rotating lamp is used, the load can be transported as a matter of course

questionTextSource: A 3-metre wide construction machine is to be transported on public roads. Under which conditions may you perform the transport?
explanationSource: With your 3-meter wide construction machine on public roads you must charge your mark and the competent authority must ask you a permit for the transport have been granted.
answerASource: The load must be labelled
answerBSource: The competent authority has issued a permit for the transport
answerCSource: The load need not be labelled

questionTextSource: A vehicle exceeds the legal limits for maximum masses and dimensions. What applies when wanting to drive this vehicle on public roads?
explanationSource: With a special permit, you can with a support vehicle was which prescribed drive. It requires an additional permit. The route can not be freely selected.
answerASource: An supplementary permit is needed
answerBSource: An escort vehicle may be prescribed
answerCSource: The route of the vehicle can be freely selected

questionTextSource: To which vehicles does the sunday and public holiday driving ban apply?
explanationSource: Trucks over 7.5 tons and trucks with trailers may not be moved on public roads on Sundays and public holidays. However, trailers may be towed behind tractors.
answerASource: Trucks with a permissible total mass exceeding 7.5 t
answerBSource: All trucks with trailers
answerCSource: Truck tractors with trailers

questionTextSource: What motor vehicles engaged in the commercial or paid transport of goods, are prohibited from driving on Sundays and public holidays?
explanationSource: The driving ban on Sundays and public holidays applies to License C, i.e. trucks with trailers or trucks with a maximum permissible mass of more than 7.5 tonnes. Cars can still drive.
answerASource: Trucks with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 7.5 t
answerBSource: Trucks towing a trailer
answerCSource: Cars towing a trailer

questionTextSource: The German summer holiday traffic regulations apply to which vehicles used in the transport of goods for hire or reward?
explanationSource: The Holiday Travel Regulation applies to trucks for the commercial or paid transport of goods with a total permissible mass of more than 7.5 t and to trucks with trailers.
answerASource: To trucks with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 7.5 t
answerBSource: To trucks with a trailer
answerCSource: To tractors

questionTextSource: You want to transport goods on a commercial basis or for payment. For which vehicles or vehicle combinations does the holiday travel regulation apply?
explanationSource: The holiday regulations apply to all trucks with a permissible total mass of over 7.5 tonnes and to trucks with trailers.
answerASource: - a maximum permissible mass of over 7.5 t
answerBSource: - a trailer
answerCSource: - a maximum permissible mass of up to 7.5 t

questionTextSource: You are entering a public road e.g. from a field or building site and considerably soil the road. Who is responsible for eliminating this traffic obstruction?
explanationSource: If you significantly pollute a road due to leaving an unpaved area, then you, the polluter, must clean it.
answerASource: You, as you have caused it
answerBSource: The competent road-building authority
answerCSource: The persons living along this section of the road

questionTextSource: What must you do to avoid endangering others by equipment or load which has fallen off your vehicle?
explanationSource: You must immediately remove any objects from the roadway that have fallen down and endanger other road users. Secure the danger zone. If necessary, contact the police or road services.
answerASource: - remove the parts yourself
answerBSource: - secure the danger spot and immediately inform the road service or police
answerCSource: - proceed and inform the nearest municipal office

questionTextSource: Your vehicle has left a considerable amount of dirt on the carriageway, causing a risk to road traffic. What do you have to do?
explanationSource: The following road users will thank you if you eliminate immediately the pollution and the danger point to the elimination doing sufficiently marked. In an easy recognition of the danger spot doing nothing is wrong.
answerASource: - clean up the dirt without delay
answerBSource: - properly indicate the point of danger until the cleaning has taken place
answerCSource: - do nothing if the point of danger is easily recognisable
